title: LedgerRow
description: One row appended to ledger.tsv per attempted transform. The JSON form here documents each tab-separated column. Keep/revert is pure checklist logic: a transform is kept iff its target check's violations strictly decreased, no other check regressed, and no guardrail was breached.

transform_id.description: e.g. 't03_canonicalize_country'; '-' for an attempt that produced no kept transform file.

target_check.description: The open check this transform targeted, e.g. 'email.regex'.

dimension.description: Dimension of the target check (reporting grouping).

viol_before.description: Target check's violation count before the transform.

viol_after.description: Target check's violation count after the transform (= before, if crashed/reverted).

regressions.description: Number of OTHER checks whose violations increased. Must be 0 for a 'keep'. '-' in the TSV when not applicable.

status.description: keep = accepted as new <best>; revert = target didn't improve / regressed another check / breached a guardrail; residual = reverted AND the target check marked unfixable (no further attempts); crash = transform errored.

rows_affected.description: Rows changed or removed by the transform.

summary.description: One-line description of the transform and outcome (no tabs).
